rationale: Article VIII, §52 restricts political subdivisions from lending credit or granting public money and governs bond issuance. It does not address homestead exemptions or property tax exemption amounts, which are governed by art. VIII, §1-b.
quote: Except as otherwise provided by this section, the Legislature shall have no power to authorize any county, city, town or other political corporation or subdivision of the State to lend its credit or to grant public money or thing of value in aid of, or to any individual, association or corporation whatsoever
_note: No conflict. §52 concerns bond issuance and credit-lending restrictions on political subdivisions, not homestead exemption levels.
